---
title: "Data Cleaning and Variable Derivation"
author: "[Author Name]"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    code_folding: show
    theme: flatly
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo    = TRUE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE
)
```

## 1. Load packages

```{r packages}
required_packages <- c("tidyverse", "here", "janitor", "skimr", "knitr")

installed <- rownames(installed.packages())
to_install <- setdiff(required_packages, installed)
if (length(to_install) > 0) install.packages(to_install)

library(tidyverse)
library(here)
library(janitor)   # clean_names(), tabyl()
library(skimr)     # skim() for quick data summaries
library(knitr)
```

---

## 2. Import raw data

```{r import}
raw <- read_csv(here("data", "acorn_data.csv"))

# Standardize column names to snake_case
raw <- clean_names(raw)

cat("Dimensions:", nrow(raw), "rows ×", ncol(raw), "columns\n")
glimpse(raw)
```

---

## 3. Initial data quality check

```{r quality-check}
# Overview of missingness, distributions, and variable types
skim(raw)
```

```{r duplicate-check}
# Check for duplicate patient identifiers
n_dupes <- raw %>%
  count(record_id) %>%       # replace patient_id with your ID variable
  filter(n > 1) %>%
  nrow()

cat("Duplicate IDs found:", n_dupes, "\n")
```

---

## 4. Apply inclusion / exclusion criteria

```{r exclusions}
# Document each exclusion step clearly for the CONSORT/flow diagram
excl_log <- list()

# Step 1: Starting cohort
excl_log[["01_starting"]] <- nrow(raw)

# Step 2: Exclude missing baseline WBC
df <- raw %>% filter(!is.na(baseline_wbc))
excl_log[["02_missing_wbc"]] <- nrow(df)

# Step 3: Exclude baseline WBC > 100
df <- df %>% filter(baseline_wbc <= 100)
excl_log[["03_wbc_gt100"]] <- nrow(df)

# Print exclusion log
excl_table <- tibble(
  Step        = names(excl_log),
  N_remaining = unlist(excl_log),
  N_excluded  = c(0, -diff(unlist(excl_log)))
)

knitr::kable(
  excl_table,
  caption = "Exclusion log",
  col.names = c("Step", "N remaining", "N excluded at this step")
)
```

---

## 5. Variable derivation and recoding

```{r recode}
#identify variables that need to be factor vars but are not character
factor_vars <- c(
  "inhosp_mortality28",
  "death14_newrrt14",
  "baseline_coma_delirium",
  "baseline_anyvasopressor",
  "newrrt28",
  "MAKE14"
  
)

#select variables of interest
df <- df %>%rename(id=record_id)%>%
                        dplyr::select(id, trt_group, inhosp_mortality28, death14_newrrt14, icufreedays28, delc14, comagdelcfreedays14, comagdelc14, persistent_renal_dysfunction28, rrtfreedays28, newrrt28, newrrt14, highest_cr_28, age, sex, race.eth, baseline_sofa, baseline_coma_delirium, incident_aki_with_onstudy_14.factor, CKD, sepsis, received_vancomycin_14, received_assigned_therapy_28, MAKE14, preill_creat_calculate, preill_creat, preill_creat_observed,
                                      baseline_vent, baseline_anyvasopressor, source_of_infection, location, priorhd, days_assigned_therapy_14, baseline_wbc, baseline_neutropenia)%>%
  mutate(n=1)%>%
  #create white race
  mutate(race_white = (ifelse(race.eth=="White, Non-Hispanic", 1, 0)))%>%
  #create CKD variable
                          mutate(CKD = ifelse(CKD==2,1,CKD))%>%
                          mutate(CKD = (ifelse(is.na(CKD),0,CKD)))%>%
  #make character variables factor
  mutate(across(where(is.character()), as.factor)) %>%
  
  #make other variables factor
  mutate(across(all_of(factor_vars), as.factor)) %>%
  #make sure incident AKI is ordered
  mutate(incident_aki_with_onstudy_14.factor = ordered(smart_abx$incident_aki_with_onstudy_14.factor,
                                                         levels = c("Alive without AKI", "Stage 1 AKI", 
                                     
                                                               "Stage 2 AKI", "Stage 3 AKI", "Died")))
  
  

df <- df %>%
  mutate(

    # --- Interaction terms ---
    T_wbc      = wbc      * trt_group,
    T_SOFA     = baseline_sofa     * trt_group,
    T_neutropenia = baseline_neutropenia * trt_group

  )
```

---

## 6. Handle missing data

```{r missingness}
# Summary of missingness in analysis variables
analysis_vars <- c(
  "mortality", "wbc", "decade", "charlson", "SOFA",
  "time_to_rx", "non_white", "T_wbc", "T_charlson", "T_SOFA"
)

missing_summary <- df %>%
  select(all_of(analysis_vars)) %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ sum(is.na(.)))) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "variable", values_to = "n_missing") %>%
  mutate(pct_missing = round(n_missing / nrow(df) * 100, 1))

knitr::kable(
  missing_summary,
  caption = "Missing data summary for analysis variables"
)
```

```{r missing-handling}
# Complete case analysis
df_analysis <- df %>% drop_na(all_of(analysis_vars))
cat("Complete cases:", nrow(df_analysis), "\n")

```

---

## 7. Final variable check

```{r final-check}
df_analysis %>%
  select(all_of(analysis_vars)) %>%
  skim()
```

---

## 8. Save analytic dataset

```{r save}
saveRDS(df_analysis, here("data", "analysis_dataset.rds"))
cat("Analytic dataset saved to data/analysis_dataset.rds\n")
cat("N =", nrow(df_analysis), "patients\n")
```

---

## 9. Session info

```{r session-info}
sessionInfo()

print("Data cleaning complete! Proceed to 02_descriptive")
```
